mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) captures aggregate demand momentum; higher growth increases corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of GDPC1 growth is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in less than 55% of high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market distress triggers reduced consumer spending and corporate margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered months when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal's t-stat falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of near-zero federal funds rates and declining consumer prices signals a liquidity trap with deficient aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL yields a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or negative returns fail to materialize in 60% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 800 thousand units indicate a post-bubble deleveraging regime where construction and household wealth contraction weigh on equities, generating negative 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55% of observations when HOUST is below 800, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% reflects acute manufacturing contraction that triggers risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow the trigger in more than 50% of instances, or the average forward return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, conventional monetary policy reaches the zero lower bound, removing future easing expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the signal's information coefficient is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production generate a joint supply-demand contraction signal that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-INDPRO interaction term achieves a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03, or negative 60-day returns fail in more than 50% of joint-decline episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The predictive power of low federal funds rates for positive equity returns is invalidated in a high unemployment regime; when UNRATE exceeds 7%, low rates coincide with negative 5-day returns.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE above 7.0%, lower FEDFUNDS levels are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, falsifying the regime-override claim.
